commit.message:
Fix plugin resolution in npm/yarn/pnpm monorepo workspaces (#426)

* Initial plan

* fix: resolve plugins from ancestor node_modules in monorepo workspaces

Walk up the entire directory tree checking each node_modules for the
plugin instead of stopping at the first package.json. This supports npm
workspaces and other monorepo setups where packages are hoisted to the
root node_modules rather than the workspace's own node_modules.

Co-authored-by: philmillman <3722211+philmillman@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: introduce WorkspaceInfo with lazy detection via AsyncLocalStorage

Replace detectJsPackageManager with a broader detectWorkspaceInfo that
also returns the workspace root path, isMonorepo flag, and monorepo tool.
Workspace detection is deferred until first access via a memoized getter
stored in AsyncLocalStorage, so no fs work occurs for commands that don't
use @plugin decorators.

Use the workspace rootPath in plugin resolution to stop walking node_modules
ancestors at the project boundary instead of the filesystem root.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: stop workspace detection walk at .git root as fallback boundary

When no lockfile is found, stop walking up the directory tree when a
.git folder is encountered rather than continuing to the filesystem root.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Refactor: Transform monolithic app.py into modular architecture

This comprehensive refactoring reduces app.py from 1,188 lines to 65 lines (94%
reduction) by splitting it into focused modules organized by responsibility.

## Key Achievements

- **Code Reduction**: 1,188 lines → 65 lines in app.py (94% reduction)
- **Eliminated Duplication**: 85% duplicate code in image generation removed
- **Tests Passing**: 59/59 tests passing (100% pass rate)
- **Modules Created**: 10 new focused modules + 3 blueprints
- **Critical Fix**: Added file locking to prevent race conditions

## New Architecture

```
tasteslikegood.com/
├── app.py (65 lines)              # Minimal entry point with app factory
├── config.py (80 lines)           # Configuration management
├── validators/
│   └── recipe_validator.py        # JSON Schema validation
├── services/                       # Business logic layer
│   ├── gemini_service.py          # AI client management
│   ├── stock_image_service.py     # Unsplash integration
│   ├── image_service.py           # AI image generation (DEDUPLICATED)
│   └── model_service.py           # Model management
├── repositories/
│   └── recipe_repository.py       # Data persistence + file locking
└── blueprints/                     # Route handlers
    ├── recipes_bp.py              # Viewing routes
    ├── generation_bp.py           # Generation routes (refactored)
    └── api_bp.py                  # API endpoints
```

## Major Improvements

1. **Single Responsibility**: Each module has one clear purpose
2. **Eliminated Duplication**: Combined duplicate image generation code
3. **Better Testability**: Services are pure Python (no Flask coupling)
4. **Prevents Race Conditions**: Added fcntl-based file locking
5. **Follows Best Practices**: Uses Flask Blueprint pattern
6. **Addresses TODOs**: Fixed duplicate code, added file locking
7. **Future-Proof**: Easy to extend with database or microservices

## Detailed Changes

### Config & Validation
- Created config.py for centralized configuration
- Created validators/ for JSON Schema validation

### Business Logic (Services)
- services/gemini_service.py: AI client with dual auth
- services/stock_image_service.py: Unsplash with 4-tier fallback
- services/image_service.py: DEDUPLICATED generate + regenerate → single function
- services/model_service.py: Model caching and refresh

### Data Layer (Repositories)
- repositories/recipe_repository.py: CRUD with file locking (fcntl)
- Prevents race conditions on concurrent writes
- TTL-based caching for list operations

### Routes (Blueprints)
- blueprints/recipes_bp.py: Homepage, recipe detail, JSON viewer
- blueprints/generation_bp.py: Refactored 175-line function into 6 focused functions
- blueprints/api_bp.py: All API endpoints consolidated

### Tests & Templates
- Updated all test imports to new module locations
- Fixed all url_for() references to use blueprint namespaces
- All 59 tests passing after refactor

## Breaking Changes

None - 100% backward compatible:
- All URL routes unchanged
- Database schema unchanged
- Authentication flow unchanged

## Documentation

See REFACTORING.md for comprehensive details including:
- Before/after architecture diagrams
- Performance improvements
- Security enhancements
- Future enhancement opportunities

commit.message:
fix(solid): correct SurfaceNormal at degenerate sphere/orb poles (#98)

* fix(solid): correct SurfaceNormal at degenerate sphere/orb poles

At the south and north poles of a G4Sphere/G4Orb, the OCCT BRep stores
degenerate vertices rather than interior face points.  TryFindClosestFace
was rejecting BRepExtrema_IsVertex solutions, so SurfaceNormal fell through
to FallbackNormal() = (0, 0, 1) instead of the true outward normal.

Two cooperating fixes in G4OCCTSolid.cc:

1. TryFindClosestFace: accept BRepExtrema_IsVertex solutions.  The
   subsequent GeomAPI_ProjectPointOnSurf step re-derives UV parameters
   independently, so the vertex location itself is not used for the normal
   evaluation.

2. TryGetOutwardNormal: extend the parametric-boundary nudge to non-periodic
   U and V boundaries (previously only periodic seams were nudged).  Sphere
   poles land at V = ±π/2 = vFirst/vLast; the tiny initial nudge places
   |dP/dU| = R·sin(δ) right at IntersectionTolerance(), which can leave
   BRepLProp_SLProps::IsNormalDefined() false.  A retry loop with
   exponentially growing nudges (10⁻³ … 10⁴ × VResolution) walks away
   from the degenerate point until the normal is well-defined.

Fixes test_geometry_validation/g4sphere-sphere-r15-v1 (south pole normal
mismatch: imported returned (0,0,1) instead of (0,0,-1)) and also resolves
the same latent issue in g4orb-orb-r11-v1.

Fix Apple Intelligence context window exhaustion after multiple games (#8)

* Fix context window exhaustion: create fresh LanguageModelSession per batch round

The session was shared across all 3 rounds, causing conversation history to
accumulate (instructions + up to 3 rounds × ~15 entries each). After several
games the total context exceeded Apple Intelligence's context window limit.

Each round is independent — deduplication is handled via the combinedAvoidWords
prompt parameter — so resetting the session between rounds has no correctness
cost and keeps each context to a single request/response pair.

fix: bootstrap history from committed stats.json and decouple downloads from traffic

Three systemic bugs causing charts to show 'Not enough history' in production:

1. History bootstrap gap: Go binary read history ONLY from .sync-cache/history.json
   (CI cache: 1 snapshot), ignoring committed src/data/stats.json (30 snapshots).
   The two history stores were completely disconnected.

   Fix: Add LoadWithBootstrap(fallbackPath string) to history/store.go. When the
   cache has fewer snapshots than src/data/stats.json, it merges them (union by
   date, cache data takes precedence for conflicts). Called from main.go instead
   of Load(). 4 new TDD tests cover: cache-empty, cache-larger, merge, missing-file.

2. Downloads gated on traffic API: The entire history snapshot (including downloads
   from the *public* Homebrew API) was gated on ts.Traffic != nil. Traffic requires
   a PAT with push access to ublue-os/*; downloads do not. If traffic ever fails,
   zero download data is recorded in history.

   Fix: Decouple download and traffic recording in main.go. pkgDownloads is built
   for every tap; traffic fields (Uniques, Count) are only set when ts.Traffic != nil.
   The history snapshot is written if either downloads exist or traffic is available.

3. Silent failure: no build-time assertion for empty charts. Fixed by adding a
   'Verify charts have data' step to daily-build.yml that fails CI if chart-empty
   appears in dist/index.html.

Verification: just build && grep -c chart-empty dist/index.html → 0
Test suite: 12 Go history tests + 28 TS tests all passing.
Go: 21+4=25 tests (12 history + 2 osanalytics + 11 tap)

commit.message:
fix: guard formula packages from cask analytics downloads

Formula packages (type=formula) can never appear in the Homebrew
cask-install API. Previously, if a cask was converted to a formula,
the old analytics value would persist indefinitely in stats.json because
the lookup in Collect() did not check the package type.

Extract download assignment to applyDownloads() helper (testable without
a live GitHub client). Add cask-type guard: formulas are skipped entirely.

Bugs fixed:
- linux-mcp-server, pmbootstrap, heic-to-dynamic-gnome-wallpaper (homebrew-tap)
- asusctl, buildstream, bluefin-cli, foundry, libvirt-full, ydotool (experimental-tap)
  all showed non-zero downloads despite being type=formula.

New tests (TestApplyDownloads_*) lock in correct behavior so future
agents cannot silently break this again.

fix: treat partial worker status as success in supervisor and automation

The worker supervisor and automation service only treated 'success' as
a successful outcome. 'partial' (worker completed session but didn't
produce a structured packet) was routed to the failure path, reclaiming
the lease and transitioning the task back to READY/FAILED.

Fix three locations:
- mapRunStatusToWorkerStatus: partial → 'completed' (was 'failed')
- Supervisor spawnWorker: success OR partial → success path
- Automation service: success OR partial → processSuccessfulWorkerResult

commit.message:
EPIC-003: SetCommand Eviction on Context Switch

twig set triggers working set computation and cache eviction when the
target item was fetched from ADO (cache miss). No eviction on cache hit.

- Inject WorkingSetService into SetCommand; call ComputeAsync(item.IterationPath)
  in both FetchedFromAdo and Found paths (DD-06: avoids redundant ADO call)
- On FetchedFromAdo only: call EvictExceptAsync(workingSet.AllIds) before sync
- On Found: skip eviction (FR-012), proceed directly to SyncWorkingSetAsync
- Replace SyncChildrenAsync with SyncWorkingSetAsync in both paths (WS-010)
- Fix sync indicator visibility (FR-015, DD-10): move work item rendering into
  buildCachedView delegate so formatted item appears above sync indicator in TTY
- Register WorkingSetService in CommandServiceModule (WS-011)
- Update SetCommand DI registration in CommandRegistrationModule (WS-012)
- Add WorkingSet value object, WorkingSetService, IWorkItemRepository.EvictExceptAsync,
  and SqliteWorkItemRepository implementation (EPIC-001 prerequisites)
- Fix: propagate CancellationToken to GetParentChainAsync(item.ParentId.Value, ct)
- Fix: TTY test uses TestConsole rendering instead of IRenderable.ToString()
- Fix: add explanatory comment on why FetchAsync proxies SyncWorkingSetAsync in
  Set_CacheHit_StillCallsSyncWorkingSet (SyncCoordinator is sealed)
- Fix: add EvictExceptAsync.DidNotReceive() to Set_Pattern_SingleMatch_SetsContext

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
